# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based Single Page Application (SPA) built with TypeScript and Vite. It follows a component-driven architecture with clear separation of concerns:

- **Frontend**: Client-side React application with routing
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL database + Auth + Real-time subscriptions)
- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state, React Context for global state
- **UI**: shadcn/ui component library built on Radix UI primitives
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens

## Key Architectural Decisions

### Authentication & Authorization
- Supabase Auth for user authentication
- Role-based access control (RBAC) implemented in `src/lib/role-access.ts`
- Route protection via `ProtectedRoute` component in `src/App.tsx`
- Password reset and invite flows handled through URL hash detection

### Data Fetching
- TanStack Query for caching, background refetching, and optimistic updates
- Supabase client configured in `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`
- Real-time subscriptions for live data updates where needed

### Component Structure
- Atomic design principles with reusable UI components in `src/components/ui/`
- Page-level components in `src/pages/` following route structure
- Feature-specific components organized by domain (employee-profile, settings, etc.)

### Form Handling
- React Hook Form for form state management
- Zod schemas for validation
- Integration with shadcn/ui form components

## Key Files and Their Purposes

### Core Application Files
- `src/App.tsx`: Main application component with routing configuration
- `src/main.tsx`: Application entry point, renders App with providers
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Root page component (likely redirects to dashboard)

### Authentication
- `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx`: Authentication context and hooks
- `src/pages/Login.tsx`: Login page
- `src/pages/SetPassword.tsx`: Password setup/reset page

### Layout and Navigation
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main application layout wrapper
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`: Top navigation bar
- `src/components/NavLink.tsx`: Custom navigation link component

### Employee Management
- `src/pages/Employees.tsx`: Employee listing page
- `src/pages/EmployeeProfile.tsx`: Individual employee profile
- `src/components/employee-profile/`: Profile tab components (Attendance, Leave, Payroll, etc.)

### Business Logic
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Role-based permissions
- `src/lib/format-date.ts`: Date formatting utilities
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave calculation logic
- `src/lib/review-schedule.ts`: Performance review scheduling
- `src/lib/notifications.ts`: Notification system

### UI Components
- `src/components/ui/`: Complete shadcn/ui component library
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Application logo component
- `src/components/SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Employee selection dropdown

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Vite build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS configuration
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `package.json`: Dependencies and scripts

## Database Schema Insights

Based on the migration files in `supabase/migrations/`, the system manages:

- Employee lifecycle (hiring, roles, departments)
- Attendance tracking with working hours and overtime
- Leave management with balances and approvals
- Payroll processing with salary components
- Performance evaluations and reviews
- Project management and client relationships
- Invoice generation and finance tracking
- HR policy documentation

## Development Workflow

- **Development Server**: `npm run dev` (runs on port 8080)
- **Build**: `npm run build` (production build)
- **Testing**: Vitest for unit tests, Playwright for e2e tests
- **Linting**: ESLint with React and TypeScript rules
- **Database**: Supabase CLI for migrations and local development

## Environment Configuration

Required environment variables (defined in `.env`):
- `VITE_SUPABASE_URL`: Supabase project URL
- `VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`: Public Supabase API key
- `VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID`: Supabase project ID

## Common Patterns

### Data Fetching
```typescript
import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query';
import { supabase } from '@/integrations/supabase/client';

const { data, isLoading } = useQuery({
  queryKey: ['entity', id],
  queryFn: async () => {
    const { data, error } = await supabase.from('table').select('*');
    if (error) throw error;
    return data;
  }
});
```

### Form Validation
```typescript
import { zodResolver } from '@hookform/resolvers/zod';
import { useForm } from 'react-hook-form';
import { z } from 'zod';

const schema = z.object({ name: z.string().min(1) });
const form = useForm({ resolver: zodResolver(schema) });
```

### Component Styling
Uses Tailwind CSS with custom CSS variables for theming:
- `--ff-body`: Body font family
- `--ff-display`: Display font family
- `--bx-success-text`: Success color
- Custom border radius variables

## Performance Considerations

- Vite for fast development builds
- React.lazy() for code splitting (not extensively used yet)
- TanStack Query for intelligent caching
- Supabase real-time subscriptions for live updates
- Optimized bundle with SWC compiler
